mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.5 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient real output that outpaces rate hikes, supporting equity valuations and generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 across all tested tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year windows during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth alongside rising retail sales during a post-shock recovery phase validates economic reopening, lifting equity risk premiums and generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark with statistical significance at the 5% level during identified post-shock recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum that correlates with short-term equity risk appetite, producing positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window for 5-day forward returns.
